behavior_summary: Provides NPC guard behavior for inspection, proximity triggers, scheduled respawn/refresh, and a multi-option talk/use dialog.
GUARD6::look: when a player 'looks at' the guard, the NPC barks a short identification line; the line differs if the NPC is dead ('dead guardsman') vs alive ('guardsman').

U8P_GUARD6::enterFastArea: when an actor enters the guard's fast trigger area, the script either restores a dead guard (clears dead/in-combat state and teleports it home) or spawns immediate reaction behaviors if the guard is alive.
U8P_GUARD6::schedule: periodic handler that spawns a scheduled check; on certain results it clears the dead flag and spawns a respawn/refresh sequence, implementing timed respawn behavior.

U8P_GUARD6::use: full talk/use interaction — presents dialog choices, reads global world-state flags (hydrosFree, pyrosFree, hasHeart, devonInRule and guard state flags), updates local/global guard flags (guard6Met, guard6Pissed, guard6Thanked), uses randomness for varied replies, and spawns other methods to perform follow-up actions (dialog sequences, animations, combat).
notable_control_flow: GUARD6::look -> calls Npc::isDead(self) and branches: if dead bark 'dead guardsman', else bark 'guardsman'.

U8P_GUARD6::enterFastArea -> calls Npc::isDead(self); if dead then Npc::clrDead, Npc::clrInCombat, Npc::teleport(self,x,y,orient,flags) to return/resurrect the NPC; else spawn immediate behavior METHODS (e.g., METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7).
U8P_GUARD6::schedule -> spawns METHOD::143A with timing params, examines its result; if the result indicates (zero) it clears dead flag (Npc::clrDead) and spawns METHOD::133F to refresh/respawn.

U8P_GUARD6::use -> early bark and spawn, then calls FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56, constructs an ask() choice list, branches on the returned choice string, reads hydrosFree/pyrosFree/hasHeart to form a small counter (use_counter) used to select alarm/doom dialog variants, and reads/writes guard state flags (guard6Met, guard6Pissed, guard6Thanked). It uses urandom to vary annoyed/greeting responses; many branches spawn additional METHODS for the resulting sequences.
open_questions: Exact behaviors implemented by spawned methods (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090) are not present in this script — their concrete effects (animation, combat, movement, detailed dialog sequences) are inferred but unknown.
Precise semantics and side effects of FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 are unclear (they appear to query/modify faction or global 'free' state used to influence dialog).
Exact meaning of some global flags (hydrosFree, pyrosFree, hasHeart) beyond their dialog influence is inferred from names and usage but not certain.
Teleport target coordinates/orientation/flags used in Npc::teleport are present but their in-world location meaning (home position vs guard post) is assumed, not proven.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD6::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an inspect/U8P_GUARD6::look event by speaking a short identification line; choose 'dead guardsman' if the NPC is dead, otherwise 'guardsman'.
entry_conditions: Event 0: player or actor examines/looks at the NPC (U8P_GUARD6::look/inspect trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_GUARD6::look' / inspect events; decide and emit a short identification bark depending on NPC dead state.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area trigger: restore dead guard (clrDead/clrInCombat/teleport) or spawn immediate reaction behaviors when an actor enters proximity.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic/scheduled check that may clear dead state and spawn respawn/refresh sequences; implements timed behavior/respawn.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' / talk dialog: present choices, read/write guard and world flags, vary replies using randomness and global state, and spawn follow-up behavior methods.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query NPC dead/alive state (used by U8P_GUARD6::look, enterFastArea, schedule).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit short spoken/written lines to a target player/process; used heavily for dialog and U8P_GUARD6::look responses.

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: Clear an NPC's dead flag (resurrect or mark alive).

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: Clear NPC's in-combat status (used when restoring a dead guard).

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Move NPC to coordinates/orientation; used to return the guard to a designated location after restoring.

function_map.name: Item::ask
function_map.role: Present dialog choice list to player and return the selected string (used by use()).

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Provide randomness for varying dialog replies.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Unknown free/global utility called from use(); appears to influence the local dialog counter (inferred to be related to faction 'free' state).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Small utility called from use() before computing dialog-counter; exact effect unclear.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn immediate reaction behavior when actor enters fast area and guard is alive (inferred: greeting, animation or combat check).
Spawn additional immediate reaction behavior for proximity entry (inferred).
Spawn scheduled check/worker; its return value determines whether to clear dead flag and respawn the NPC.
Spawn respawn/refresh method after clearing dead in schedule().
Spawn follow-up behavior/dialog sequence after a use/talk branch (inferred: extended dialog or action).
Spawn a panic/doom sequence chosen by use() based on world flags and randomness (inferred).

external_calls.purpose: Check if NPC is dead to choose which bark line to use.
Emit the short identification line to the player.
Decide whether to restore NPC or run alive behavior.
Clear dead state when restoring the NPC on area entry.
Clear in-combat flag so restored NPC is not flagged as fighting.
Move the restored NPC to a designated coordinates/orientation (return to post/home).
Query or modify a global 'free' state used to influence dialog selection (inferred to relate to faction freedom flags).
Call an auxiliary utility before computing dialog-counter; exact purpose unclear.
Present the player with a list of dialog choices and obtain the selected string.
Generate randomness to vary replies when guard6Pissed or other random branches are active.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used to increment a local dialog counter that selects among alarm/doom dialog variants; likely indicates faction/state 'Hydros is free'.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used to increment the same local dialog counter influencing alarm dialog; likely indicates 'Pyros is free'.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Read to alter which alarm/earth-shake dialog variant is used; acts as a special world-state modifier.

reads.name: guard6Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to detect whether this guard has met the player before and select introductory vs repeat dialog.

reads.name: guard6Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose between calm or annoyed dialog variants; toggled by the use() branches.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Read to tailor 'What is your duty?' and 'Where am I?' responses depending on whether Devon is present in the palace/rule.

writes.name: guard6Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set or cleared by dialog branches to mark the guard as annoyed or calmed down.

writes.name: guard6Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to true the first time the player successfully meets/talks to this guard to change future dialog behavior.

writes.name: guard6Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set once when the guard gives a one-time thank-you line to prevent repetition of that message.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides NPC guard behavior for inspection, proximity triggers, scheduled respawn/refresh, and a multi-option talk/use dialog.
